title: Multi-Dataset Robustness Summary
purpose: DUA-safe aggregate summary comparing MIMIC-IV strict clinical-event evidence with eICU second-dataset outcome-proxy evidence.
top_line: MIMIC-IV established strict clinical-event cross-cohort retrospective stability, while eICU added a separate second-dataset outcome-proxy check; across both datasets, ERA preserved the same threshold-direction behavior: lower thresholds increased detection, while conservative thresholds reduced review burden and false positives.
lead_time_context: At the conservative t=6.0 operating point, MIMIC-IV showed 4 hrs median lead-time context across locked cross-cohort evidence, while eICU showed 3.41 hrs median lead-time context in the outcome-proxy check.

mimic_iv.role: Locked strict clinical-event cross-cohort validation release
mimic_iv.cohort_label: Full validation cohort + two deterministic case-level subcohorts
mimic_iv.interpretation: Strict clinical-event retrospective validation; conservative setting optimized for low-burden review queues.

eicu.interpretation: Outcome-proxy retrospective check using mortality/discharge-derived event context; not directly comparable to MIMIC clinical-event detection.

comparison.shared_behavior: Across both datasets, lower thresholds increased detection, while conservative thresholds reduced review burden and false positives.
comparison.not_apples_to_apples: MIMIC-IV uses stricter clinical-event labels; eICU uses outcome-proxy labels from mortality/discharge-derived event context. Detection rates should not be compared as equivalent endpoint definitions.
comparison.safe_claim: Cross-dataset retrospective robustness evidence across de-identified ICU datasets.

mimic_iv_physionet.label: MIMIC-IV v3.1 PhysioNet citation
mimic_iv_physionet.apa: Johnson, A., Bulgarelli, L., Pollard, T., Gow, B., Moody, B., Horng, S., Celi, L. A., & Mark, R. (2024). MIMIC-IV (version 3.1). PhysioNet. RRID:SCR_007345. https://doi.org/10.13026/kpb9-mt58

mimic_iv_scientific_data.label: MIMIC-IV Scientific Data publication
mimic_iv_scientific_data.apa: Johnson, A. E. W., Bulgarelli, L., Shen, L., et al. (2023). MIMIC-IV, a freely accessible electronic health record dataset. Scientific Data, 10, 1. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41597-022-01899-x

eicu_physionet.label: eICU v2.0 PhysioNet citation
eicu_physionet.apa: Pollard, T., Johnson, A., Raffa, J., Celi, L. A., Badawi, O., & Mark, R. (2019). eICU Collaborative Research Database (version 2.0). PhysioNet. RRID:SCR_007345. https://doi.org/10.13026/C2WM1R

eicu_scientific_data.label: eICU Scientific Data publication
eicu_scientific_data.apa: Pollard, T. J., Johnson, A. E. W., Raffa, J. D., Celi, L. A., Mark, R. G., & Badawi, O. (2018). The eICU Collaborative Research Database, a freely available multi-center database for critical care research. Scientific Data. https://doi.org/10.1038/sdata.2018.178

physionet_standard.label: PhysioNet standard citation
physionet_standard.apa: Goldberger, A., Amaral, L., Glass, L., Hausdorff, J., Ivanov, P. C., Mark, R., ... & Stanley, H. E. (2000). PhysioBank, PhysioToolkit, and PhysioNet: Components of a new research resource for complex physiologic signals. Circulation, 101(23), e215–e220. RRID:SCR_007345.

dua.label: PhysioNet Credentialed Health Data Use Agreement 1.5.0
dua.summary: Credentialed restricted data requires avoiding re-identification attempts, avoiding disclosure of identities in publications or communications, and not sharing restricted-data access.

public_policy: Aggregate public evidence only. Raw restricted files and row-level derived outputs remain local-only.
notice: Decision support only. Retrospective aggregate analysis only. Not intended to diagnose, direct treatment, replace clinician judgment, or independently trigger escalation.
